commands.author: fuller-stack-dev
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2d1631ba33ef524f249ea8da68d658ae9fdad307)
commands.review_summary:
This PR updates the OpenRouter catalog pricing converter to parse string or numeric USD-per-token prices and replaces the OpenRouter tests with string, numeric, zero, and malformed-price cases.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and live-data inspection: current main only accepts numeric OpenRouter prices, while the live OpenRouter catalog currently returns priced models with string prompt/completion values, so the current guard accepts none.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer review is needed for the exported numeric-catalog compatibility choice; the remaining blocker is not merely missing contributor proof or a straightforward code-only repair.

target.title: Fix OpenRouter pricing map coming back empty (per-token string values)
target.body:
**Problem.** `fetchOpenRouterPricingMap` / `openRouterPricingMapFromCatalog` return an empty map for real OpenRouter data.

OpenRouter's `/api/v1/models` returns each price as a USD-**per-token** numeric **string**, e.g. for `anthropic/claude-opus-4.8`:

```json
"pricing": { "prompt": "0.000005", "completion": "0.000025" }
```

(`0.000005 ├Ś 1e6 = $5 / 1M tokens`, the correct price.)

The current code only accepts `typeof === "number"`, so every entry fails the guard and is skipped ŌĆö the map is always empty. Separately, it runs values through `pricingFromUsdPerMillion` (├Ę1e6), which would make an already-per-token value 1,000,000├Ś too low if a number ever reached it.

**Fix.** Parse `prompt`/`completion` as USD-per-token (string or number) and map them straight through with `pricingFromUsdPerToken`. This matches the LiteLLM path (`input_cost_per_token` used directly) and the README, which lists OpenRouter as a per-token pricing source. Free models (`"0"`) stay at `$0`; malformed entries are skipped.

**Real-behavior proof.** Calling `fetchOpenRouterPricingMap` against the live `openrouter.ai/api/v1/models` endpoint:

Before (`main`):
```
priced_models: 0
anthropic/claude-opus-4.8: null
openai/gpt-4o-mini: null
```

After (this PR):
```
priced_models: 340
anthropic/claude-opus-4.8: {"inputUsdPerToken":0.000005,"outputUsdPerToken":0.000025}
openai/gpt-4o-mini: {"inputUsdPerToken":1.5e-7,"outputUsdPerToken":6e-7}
```

The helper goes from an empty map to pricing every model, with values matching OpenRouter's published rates ($5 / $25 per 1M for opus-4.8; $0.15 / $0.60 per 1M for gpt-4o-mini).

**Verification.** New tests fail on `main` (string ŌćÆ empty map; number ŌćÆ 1e6├Ś off) and pass after the change. `pnpm check` (format, lint, type-aware lint, typecheck, coverage) is green.

Note: the previous test fed numeric inputs and asserted a per-million conversion ŌĆö neither matches the live API ŌĆö so it's replaced with string/number per-token cases plus free/malformed coverage.

Used the Claude CLI while working on this.
target.author: devyrpauli
